steps.title: Find the shown line
steps.content:
The graph of $y=f(x)-19$ passes through $(-5,-3)$ and $(0,-8)$. Its slope is
$$\frac{-8-(-3)}{0-(-5)}=-1$$
and its $y$-intercept is $-8$, so
$$y=-x-8$$

steps.title: Recover the function
steps.content:
Since the graphed function is $y=f(x)-19$, add $19$ to both sides.
$$f(x)-19=-x-8$$
$$f(x)=-x+11$$

steps.title: Match the positive constants
steps.content: The function $f(x)=-x+11$ can be written as $f(x)=d-cx$ with $d=11$ and $c=1$, both positive. The answer is A.
